_comment: Hand-curated overrides for tip sections — used when the catalog body is too dense to render cleanly. Keyed by tip id. Re-runs of parse-catalog.ts merge these in.
operational-deployment-by-archetype:
**Anchor dishes** — fund the operation.
- Dal makhani, mole poblano, mujadara, hummus, gigantes plaki. Under $1.50 cost, hold 3+ days, plate in <5 min.

**Showcase plates** — drive press and check average.
- Beef Wellington ($45–52), Bistecca Fiorentina ($48–58), Picanha ($32–38), Soupe VGE ($42–55). Built around branded faux-meat.

**Faux-meat rule** — lead with the brand when the dish *is* the meat (picanha, Big Mac). Keep tofu/legume primary when the dish *is* the legume (mapo tofu, falafel); offer the faux-meat as an upcharge.
foodservice-sourcing-summary:
**Chunk Foods** — Sysco, Chefs' Warehouse, or Webstaurant Store. 18-month frozen shelf life is the unlock.

**Juicy Marbles** — direct via juicymarbles.com Food Service, or Webstaurant Store (4 oz × 20/case).

**Beyond & Impossible** — Sysco, US Foods, GFS, Restaurant Depot, Webstaurant. ~$7/lb wholesale floor on ground SKUs. Backbone of any ground-protein rebuild.
menu-engineering-tips-for-vegan-restaura:
1. **Price the Picanha proudly.** Vegan picanha at $32–38 with Juicy Marbles reads as premium — the cut size is real. Offer a $19–22 Beyond Steak Tips entry tier alongside.
2. **Bundle bulk-prep into combo platters.** Ethiopian, Indian, Brazilian combos sell three $0.95–1.40 batch items at $18–22.
3. **Fast-service drives lunch volume.** Pad thai, banh mi, gazpacho, hummus, falafel, bibimbap, mac & cheese — under 8 min from prep. Your $13–17 lunch tier.
4. **Use 🥘 to plan prep.** Anything tagged 🥘 belongs on a Mon/Wed/Fri rotating bulk-prep schedule.
5. **Two-tier where the faux-meat alt is meaningful.** "Bibimbap $15 / with Chunk Steak $22" — +$5–9 upsells, no extra labor.
6. **Quote faux-meat quarterly.** Impossible and Beyond have cut foodservice prices ~15–20% twice since 2021; the category is still volatile.
